segments.text:
Psyche Relics
A Look Into Psychedelics and Their Effects on Brain Chemistry

INTRODUCTION
Hallucinogens include, but not limited to: LSD, DMT, and psilocybin (‘magic’) mushrooms
General Effects:
Euphoria
Intense visual and auditory hallucinations
Skewed sense of time
Impaired depth perception
Effects can last from 4-12 hours
Interfere with the production of serotonin
DMT
(raw form)
Lysergic Acid
Paper tabs are
Coated in LSD
And taken whole

Background and Classifcation
Many psychedelics are found as, derived from, or are close analogues to allelochemicals produced by certain fungi and plants. These are defense chemicals
Most psychedelics are considered a Schedule I drugs
There are a number of ways the drugs are classified. One way is by what neurotransmitter the drug structurally resembles
E.g Serotonergic - imitates or enhances the effects of serotonin. These include some common ones such as DMT, LSD, and psilocybin

Applications of Psychedelics
Historically have been used for ritualistic and medicinal purposes
Have relatively recently become popular as a recreational drug around the 1960s
Some drugs classified as psychedelics are used for medical purposes. Ketamine for instance is used as a surgery anesthetic.
Research is currently being done in psychotherapy for the treatment of various mental disorders such as PTSD

Lysergic Acid Diethylamide (LSD)
Common Names:
Acid, Dots, Mellow Yellow
Derived from poisonous Rye fungus ergot
Ergot is an alkaloid
Precipitates in a white powder form that is water soluble
Common Effects:
Intense Euphoria
Auditory/Visual Hallucinations
McClure, J. (2020, April 17). Bicycle Day: Albert Hofmann's 1st Time Tripping on Acid-On Purpose: DoubleBlind.

LSD Chemistry
C 10 H 25 N 3 O
Semi-synthetic
2 stereogenic centers
C5 Position and C8 Position
C-N bond broken at chiral center
Creates carbonic acid functional group
Various chemicals + heat used to break C-OH bond in carbonic acid group
4 possible isomers
Only one isomer considered effective as a psychoactive
Wilcox, A. (2020, July 24). How LSD is Made. Retrieved November 21, 2020, from https://doubleblindmag.com/how-lsd-is-made/

S conf.
R conf.
SN2 Reaction
Lone pair of electrons donated

Psilocybin
Psilocybin - A tryptamine alkaloid, isolated from a number   ‘                    of fungi including the genus psilocybe
Can be made by E-coli
1957 Life magazine article “Seeking the Magic Mushroom,” by Gordon Wassen. [11]
Chemically isolated and structure determined by Albert Hofmann in 1958.
“The tryptamines, including psilocybin, bear a striking resemblance to human neurochemistry. The human brain, and indeed most nervous systems, run partially on 5-hydroxytryptamine, also known as serotonin.” [12]
Phosphate group
Methylated amine
[3-[2-(dimethylamino)ethyl]-1H-indol-4-yl] dihydrogen phosphate
Tryptamine

Metabolism:
(Psychedelics)
(Neurotransmitters)
Water
Mao (Monoamine Oxidase)
Psilocin O-glucuronide
(ALDH) Aldehyde Dehydrogenase
UDP
[13,14]
80% excreted in this form
alpha-C
oxi level
1
2
3

(psilocybe cubensis)
Mushroom blueing rxn
PsiP ---> hydrolysis
PsiL ---> oxidation
Radical oligomerization
[15]

25I-NBOMe
One of a family of synthetic psychoactive substances called NBOMes
Derived from 2C family of phenethlamine drugs
Contain a N-(2-methoxy)benzyl substituent
(Dean et al. 2013, Souza et al. 2018, Zawilska et al. 2020)

25I-NBOMe History
NBOMes synthesized and described by Alexander Shulgin, 1991
25I-NOMe developed by Ralf Heim, 2003
Used for research purposes
Recreational use first recorded in 2010
Sold online as LSD alternative
Many names (N-bomb, Smiles, Solaris, etc .)
(Bersani et al. 2013, Eshleman et al. 2018, Shulgin 1991)

25I-NBOMe Effects
Mental
Audio/Visual hallucinations
Confusion
Paranoia/Anxiety
Aggression
Physiological
Increased temperature
Rapid heart beat
Muscle spasms
Nausea/Vomiting
Difficulty breathing
Loss of consciousness
Seizures
Strong agonist of 5-HT2A serotonin receptors
Overdose effects: organ failure, heart attack, respiratory failure, brain aneurysm, coma, death.
(Caldicott et al. 2013, The Foundation for a Drug-Free World 2015, World Health Organization 2014)

25I-NBOMe Structure
Formula: C 18 H 22 INO 3
Full name: 4 - iodo - 2,5-dimethoxy - N -( 2-methoxy benzl ) phenethylamine
(World Health Organization 2014)

25I-NBOMe Synthesis
Alkylation
Imine intermediate
Reduction w/ NaBH 4
(Braden et al. 2006, Grane 2011, Heim 2003, Kamińska et al. 2020)

Thanks for listening
Any Questions?
